reasoning: While the fidelity composite score of 0.88 suggests approval, the programmatic shingling recall (0.845) falls below the 0.85 threshold, corroborating the LLM's identification of significant text omissions on pages 6-8. Specifically, the loss of jurisdiction names and technical sub-bullets in a government document regarding emergency infrastructure warrants human intervention. Given that a false positive is 3x more costly and the structural score is low (0.68), human review is necessary to restore the missing data and nested list hierarchies.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There are several significant text omissions in the presentation slides sections (pages 6, 7, and 8). On page 6, all sub-bullets for 'Military Sites Update' and 'Cybersecurity Remediation' are missing (e.g., details about Seymour Johnson AFB and the Ad hoc committee). On page 7, the specific details for the 4 jurisdictions (Cleveland, Lincoln, Onslow, Polk), the Rutherford project, and the population/land mass statistics are omitted. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 6: 'Seymour Johnson AFB is working on a multi year contract...', 'Good conversation with ECI/NIWC...', 'Ad hoc committee has been created...', 'Ad hoc committee discussing...'. Page 7: Sub-bullets for Cleveland County, Lincoln County, Onslow County, Polk County; 'One active RFAI to i3 projects (Rutherford)'; 'Total Population served by NG 911...', 'Total land mass covered by NG911...'. concerns.output_quote: The output entirely omits these sub-bullets, retaining only the parent list items at element index 44 (Page 6), 49 (Page 7), and 59 (Page 8).

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There is a minor text error in the transcription of a legal statute on page 5. The source cites 'G.S. 138A-15', whereas the output incorrectly cites 'G.S. 138-15', omitting the 'A'.
concerns.location: Ethics Awareness/Conflict of Interest Statement section, Page 5, element index 29.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Level 1 (H1) is correctly used for document titles (Agenda at index 1, Minutes at index 10). However, on Page 8, 'NMAC (Greg Dotson)' is assigned H1 (index 58), which is a sub-topic of the agenda and should be an H2 to match equivalent topics like 'AT&T/Intrado Issue Update' (index 60) and 'Closing Remarks' (index 61). Additionally, the supporting documents section starting on Page 5 lacks an H1 title and immediately begins with H2 (index 27).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The topics 'NMAC', 'AT&T/Intrado Issue Update', and 'Closing Remarks' are all top-level agenda items listed on Page 1.
concerns.output_quote: Index 58 is a heading level 1 (H1) for 'NMAC (Greg Dotson)', while equivalent items at indices 60 and 61 are assigned heading level 2 (H2).

concerns.analysis: The output fails to preserve the hierarchical nesting of lists found in the source. In multiple sections (GIS Update, Statistics, NMAC, Military Sites), the source uses a main bullet point followed by nested sub-bullets. The output either omits the sub-bullets entirely or renders the list as flat, losing the structural relationship. For example, under 'GIS Update' (Page 7), four specific jurisdictions are listed as sub-bullets, but these are missing in the output (index 49).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: On Page 7, under 'GIS Update (Tom Rogers)', there is a bullet '4 jurisdictions remain...' with four nested sub-bullets (Cleveland, Lincoln, Onslow, Polk).
concerns.output_quote: The list at element index 49 contains '4 jurisdictions remain to reach 100% i3 readiness (as of 5/31/2022).' but the nested sub-items are missing.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains text that functions as URLs, such as 'arcgis.com' on Page 7. While the text is captured in the output (index 51), it is not formatted as a functional, clickable hyperlink.

Fix the heading hierarchy for 'NMAC (Greg Dotson)' and ensure lists are correctly nested rather than flattened.
